##NAMES=Seaborgite
##RRUFFID=R250084
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=LiK_2_Na_6_(UO_2_)(SO_4_)_5_(SO_3_OH)(H_2_O)
##LOCALITY=Blue Lizard mine, Red Canyon, San Juan County, Utah, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Secondary phase of sprays of light-yellow, long flattened prisms or blades found on gypsum in association with copiapite, ferrinatrite, ivsite, metavoltine, and römerite. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2021)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250084
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Li_0.79_Na_5.02_K_2.02_(UO_2_)(SO_4_)_5_(SO_3_OH)(H_2_O) ; The empirical formula using Na measured via LA-ICP-MS is Li_0.79_Na_5.19_K_2.02_(UO_2_)(SO_4_)_5_(SO_3_OH)(H_2_O).  The empirical formula using Li, Na, and K based on the structurenrefinement is Li_1.00_Na_5.81_K_2.19_(UO_2_)(SO_4_)_5_(SO_3_OH)(H_2_O).
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.4511 b: 14.487 c: 15.8735 alpha: 76.295 beta: 81.439 gamma: 85.511 volume: 1203.07 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
